---
title: "Join us / Visit"
citation: false
toc: false
comments: false
format: 
  html: 
    page-layout: full
title-block-banner: false
---

We are always looking for motivated people to work with. The Circadia Lab sits at the intersection of chronobiology, neuroimaging, signal processing, and open research infrastructure — if that sounds like your kind of problem space, we'd love to hear from you.

## PhD opportunities

We welcome applications from prospective PhD students interested in chronobiology, sleep science, neuroimaging, actigraphy-based methods, or the development of open research software. Projects typically combine data analysis and software development with a substantive scientific question, and we place a strong emphasis on reproducible, open research practice.

We welcome applications from candidates with their own funding or who are applying to external schemes. We strongly encourage informal contact before submitting a formal application — please get in touch to discuss whether your interests align with current lab directions.

## Postdoctoral fellowships

We do not routinely advertise postdoctoral positions, but we actively support strong candidates in applying for independent fellowship funding to join the lab. Relevant schemes include:

- [Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowships](https://marie-sklodowska-curie-actions.ec.europa.eu/actions/postdoctoral-fellowships) (EU/international)
- [Leverhulme Early Career Fellowships](https://www.leverhulme.ac.uk/early-career-fellowships)
- [BBSRC](https://www.ukri.org/councils/bbsrc/), [MRC](https://www.ukri.org/councils/mrc/), and [EPSRC](https://www.ukri.org/councils/epsrc/) fellowship schemes

If you are interested in developing a fellowship application with us as the host institution, please reach out with your CV and a brief outline of your research interests.

## Visiting researchers

We welcome visiting students and researchers for short stays. If you are interested in a research visit, please contact us to discuss logistics and possible collaborative projects.

## Get in touch

Reach us at `lucas.franca` and `mario.miguel` (at) northumbria.ac.uk.
